content.text: for the first time in almost 10 years
Google started to lose users while at
the same time AI search tools are on the
rise this has led to a mad scramble as
Google tries to catch up and add their
own AI overview features okay Don't
Panic it's not clear yet which platform
will win Google has the funding and lots
of experience of reverse engineering
other platforms or simply buying them
but one thing we do know for sure is
that if we want to keep getting organic
traffic then we need to learn how to
optimize for AI search the good news is
it's pretty easy to do in this video
I'll show you exactly why people prefer
to use AI search tools how AI search
chooses the results it shares how to
optimize right now for every AI tool
regardless of which wins and how to use
chat GPT prompts to help you to do this
so let's dive in but to understand why
people are moving to AI search I need to
tell you about my weekend so last
weekend a new computer game came out
that had me pulling out my hair in
frustration it's in open Beta right now
which means you can buy the game but
it's still being tweaked and fine-tuned
and it's a little harder than it should
be I played for a few hours and I
thought I was doing pretty good but
after around 30 minutes of trying to
kill the insanely difficult first boss I
gave in and I checked out Google for
tips on how to beat it the problem of
course is that nothing worked the advice
was generic and the character I was
playing was too weak to survive the
techniques that they suggested so I
grinded a few more levels to see if I
could win by simply out gearing the
fight but still no luck suddenly it
occurred to me that if I went into the
search functions in chat GPT otherwise
known as search gbt I can ask a more
specific question and possibly get a
more relevant answer so I did did just
that and I found out exactly what I
needed to do for my character class and
my level and even shared a video I
hadn't seen recommended before which
helped me to finally beat it this is why
people are moving to AI search and why
chat GPT has over 300 million users a
week right now and perplexity secured
$250 million in funding because not only
does AI search allow for a more
conversational search query where you
can be super specific and detailed about
your needs but the tool can actually
understand the context of that question
and provide the right answer that fits
often pulling from multiple sources to
create that custom answer compare that
to a classic search engine that gives a
basic page of search results where you
have to scan through all the options and
keep clicking back and forth to find
anything and it's just a night and day
difference in user experience the good
news though is that even with the rise
in AI search traditional SEO isn't dead
mainly because these AI search tools
work off of the backbone of SEO huz in
fact optimizing for AI search is fairly
easy as long as you've been following
Google's eat guidelines and they good
content guidelines you also need to add
in some technical SEO which you probably
stop doing so let's break this all down
so AI search works off of a three tiered
logic system that's focused on providing
the best answer to the user if content
directly answers the user's query and
also ranks well based on traditional SEO
signals like Authority eat backlinks AI
will prioritize this content first as
the result simply because it's already a
known trusted Source because it ranks on
Google Bing or whatever search engine it
normally provides a direct concise
relevant answer and it's been validated
by human search Behavior that's why it's
ranking here's where it gets interesting
though what about if it can't find
content that ranks so if AI finds
content that directly fits the user's
question but it doesn't rank highly in
traditional SEO it will still share it
with the user why well AI systems for AI
search don't care about page rank as
much as they do about relevance and fit
so if a blog post perfectly answers the
user query but is on page three it can
still be featured in that AI overview
again assuming there's no ranking better
alternative but what about if neither of
these exist well when there's no exact
answer available AI will look for close
matches that rank highly it's a backup
strategy instead of pulling the perfect
answer it will try and extract content
from related content that ranks the
highest this is because AI always wants
to try and provide an answer even if
it's not perfect instead of saying I
don't know it wants to give a relevant
approximation so what does this all mean
for us and our content marketing well
basically the better you know your
audience topics and the more Niche it is
the more specific it is the better
however the quality of the content and
backlinks are still important which is
great for traditional SEO efforts that
you're probably doing already speaking
of content quality though there does
seem to be a preference in AI search
towards that eat style it's content that
has unique data points experiences
expertise and references now there could
be a few reasons for this first off
these small differences stand out
against a sea of CopyCat content on a
topic adding the fact that any relevant
and unique information to to the answer
improves the quality of the search
result and that's why they might
reference you finally it also helps your
content to possibly be more in line with
the user searches so if you're talking
about more specific details and others
don't cover again it's all stuff you
should already be doing we just need to
be smart about how we apply this and
optimize our content for AI search you
see because AI search is contextual it
very rarely pulls single pieces of
information from a search result instead
it will pull the sentence or paragraph
around the answer as well this is
because this helps the reader to
understand the con text around the
answer which is a better user experience
knowing all this how do we apply that
well again it's just good writing habits
you need to have clear section headings
and subheads you need to make sure you
cover the relevant information for that
section and the really easy way to do
that is to use the what why how and show
this gives that Clarity and context that
AI search is looking for so what are we
talking about in this section why does
it matter why would it matter to the
reader how does it work how do they do
it show them also add images video
things like that super simple if you do
that not only will your writing improve
but you'll also be a great Contender for
search GPT perplexity and also Google
overviews when they start to add these
for all searches however there's one
more thing you need to do but if I'm
honest you probably used to do and
stopped at some point last year and
that's schema markup so what is this and
why do we care about it so schema markup
is basically additional code that helps
search engines fully understand what a
page is about it was built years ago and
was a combined effort from Google Bing
Yahoo and Yandex right very they
actually should be sending alarm bells
in your
brain cuz when multiple billion dollar
competing companies agree on something
you should pay attention even if it
seems boring for example marketers soon
realized that if you added schema markup
to a post or page you are almost
guaranteed a jump in the Impressions and
a movement of the servs which then later
being featured for Rich Snippets if you
don't know what they are rich Snippets
are those search results that you see
that go into more detail at the top of
the page sometimes they're kind of like
the old school version of AI overviews
today the really cool thing about them
is they would often feature pages with
the better schema as a result at the top
of the page instead of featuring the
page with the most backlinks that meant
you could steal position one even if you
technically ranked in position 10
however most people stopped adding
schema in August of 2023 when Google
announced they were removing its
relevance outside of certain industries
the thing is though since then a lot has
changed and it turns out that all search
AI platforms use schema to fully
understand the context of a page and its
contents not only that but with Google's
traffic going down being able to be seen
better by other search engines that
never stopped using schema is a good
thing especially when you realize that
search GPT chat GPT is actually running
on the Bing search engine in the
background that means when it searches
for results and answers it's searching
Bing and not Google so it probably makes
sense to rank there right so yeah schema
is pretty important again but the
question of course is how do we create
this schema code well there's a few
options you can do it manually with
tools like the schema markup generator
basically you choose which particular
type of schema you want and then you
fill out all the details it's a bit time
intensive but you can fully customize it
with these tools adding in emojis and
spacing and all that kind of stuff then
you have plugins like rank math or yast
for WordPress sites that do this
automatically however these are not
perfect you see most articles require
three types of schema you have breakr
which is basic it just shows how the
page URL fits into your site structure
so it would be website uh website blog
website blog article name then you have
fakeu schema and this is where you
answer each major question that appears
in your article and then finally you
have article and article body schema
this is where these plug-in tools fail
you see the article body schema is
basically an exact copy of your entire
article broken down into code so that
these tools can scan the entire post and
understand all of it issue though with
the plugins is that they only write the
schema for the introduction of your
article and nothing else they're cutting
out around 95% of their article from the
scan which is not great when you're only
doing this so that AI search can find it
so what can you do well there's a few
Solutions you could just grab the entire
post copy it use the schema tool I
mentioned paste the article in get the
code and you could just add it back into
one of these plugins super easy if
you're using that plugin and WordPress
however let's be honest 40% of websites
don't use WordPress and they don't have
these plug-in options which means you're
kind of stuck doing them all manually as
you can probably guess this is
incredibly time intensive especially if
you've got to do it for every page
however you don't want to miss out on it
because of all the benefits good news
for you is I wrote a prompt that can
help you do this for much faster with
chat GPT you can click the link in the
description below and I'll email it to
you or try and pause the video and just
write it out yourself if you want so
here's how it works the prompt is going
to create the three schemas for you one
after another however because writing
schema code is fairly memory intensive
it can cause some issues with chat GPT
if we try and do this all at once so
here's how to get around it we enter the
core prompt and then the tool will ask
you for the URL of the page that you
want to create schema for so we often
add schema last as the final part of our
upload or chances are high you've never
added it so you want to add it to an
article so that's totally fine you enter
the URL and it'll give you the
breadcrumb schema then go ahead and open
up notepad on your computer and copy the
schema across then hit enter so you
create a blank line Gap underneath that
code in your notepad we do this because
we're going to be adding more code
sections below this and we don't want to
cause any issues which can happen if
everything's on the same line once
you've done that go back to chat gbt and
tell the tool to continue it will then
create the article body section and ask
you for a few details so it wants the
image URL of your website logo basically
where it's saved on your website it'll
want the URL of the featured article
image the thing that's at the top of the
page it want the author's name and the
publication date the tool will try and
from The Prompt it'll try and grab them
for you but it doesn't always work so
you'll often have to grab these manually
but that's really easy for the logo
simply go into the media section of your
website search for your logo find the
url that's relevant to it and that's all
you need if you want to check it you can
open that URL and you'll see a page will
open with just a logo file like you can
see here for the featured image file
simply right click on it in your article
and click copy image link the rest of
the stuff you can madly add and type in
the tool will then give you the article
schema so copy and paste that into the
notepad however you'll see for the body
section it says add article body here in
between quotation marks I've done this
on purpose because the talk can struggle
to grab the entire article on its own
it'll often miss things again just
grabbing the introduction on something
it's far easier for you to copy your
entire article highlight it and then
paste it in in notepad in between those
quotation marks if you've been working
for Google do it's even easier so if
you've got your draft over there you
know highlight all copy paste across
again once you've done that make sure
there's a gap below this new code in
your notepad for the final part we're
going to add go back over to chat gbt
and let the tool know you've copied it
across and it'll continue with the third
schema which is the FAQ basically it's
going to scan your entire article for
the questions that show up in the post
so far and the answers that you gave to
that this part is key to AI search
because obviously people are asking a
lot of questions however full disclosure
in that the tool can have issues here
either it doesn't find all the questions
that you've got in your post or it tries
to rewrite them into generic smaller
answers this is not what we want when
you went to the effort to add depth and
expertise and experience right we want
that generic eat the prompt does tell it
not to do do this but sometimes it will
still be a pain and make this mistake so
here's how to get around it before it
writes the code the tool should list all
the FAQ questions and answers V it found
in the chat first all you need to do is
read through them and make sure they're
correct and not missing anything if they
are simply tell it to do them correctly
and not change the answers tell them you
need them exactly as it is it might take
one or two tries but then it'll finally
figure it out so once it gives you the
correct questions and answers tell it to
continue and it'll give you the final
code section and you just need to copy
that into your notepad and you have all
three of them ready to be added to your
page there just a few minor things to do
now first of all we just want to check
the code the combined three codes all in
one so grab them all and make sure
there's not any issues we're going to go
across to Google's Rich results testing
tool enter the code in there the
combined code of all three and then just
hit run if there are any problems it
will let you know often this is just a
line issue and you need to add a space
and you'll see where it is in the code
if it works you should see the two
schemas highlighted next you just need
to add that into your article so all
platforms are different but you'll
usually see a Json code section add it
here and hit save finally you just need
to make sure you reindex your pages in
both Google and Bing CNS Bing is what
chat gbt runs on this way it'll make the
article rank faster if you've never done
it before you literally enter the URL
and request indexing and boom your
content is now far more likely to show
up in AI search super easy right however
just because AI search is the future
